vehicleProfile.notes: Front-wheel-drive vehicle. No 4Motion, Haldex coupling, rear differential, rear driveshaft, bevel box, or AWD fluid service applies.

components.engine: EA888 Gen 3B 2.0L TSI turbocharged direct-injection four-cylinder
components.transmission: Aisin 8-speed automatic; verify exact transmission code from vehicle build information before ordering service parts
components.engineOilCapacityWithFilter: Approximately 5.7 L / 6.0 qt; verify final dipstick level
components.oilFilter: Cartridge filter; confirm current OE or Mann-equivalent part by VIN
components.coolant: Volkswagen-approved G12evo/G13-compatible coolant only; do not mix chemistries without confirming compatibility
components.driveConfiguration: FWD only; front CV axles and front-wheel-bearing inspection apply

services.name: Engine Oil & Cartridge Filter Change
tools: Fluid extractor or correct drain-plug tool
materials: VIN-confirmed cartridge filter kit with new housing O-ring
steps: Warm the engine briefly, park level, and allow components to cool enough to work safely.
Extract oil through the dipstick tube or drain through the factory drain point using the correct procedure.
Remove the top-mounted cartridge housing, replace the filter and housing O-ring, and lubricate the O-ring with fresh oil.
Torque the filter housing to the factory specification; do not overtighten the plastic housing.
Refill with approximately 5.7 L of VW 508 00 oil, start briefly, inspect for leaks, and set final level on the dipstick.
Reset the service reminder through the infotainment or instrument-cluster service menu.
services.tips: Use oil carrying the actual VW 508 00 approval, not merely a bottle labeled 'synthetic' or 'European formula.' The turbocharger and variable valve timing system depend on correct oil chemistry.
services.whatItDoes: Replaces contaminated oil and the cartridge filter that protect the turbocharged EA888 engine.
services.whyItMatters: Correct VW-approved oil protects turbocharger bearings, timing components, cam adjusters, and internal engine surfaces.
services.ifDelayed: Old or incorrect oil increases sludge, turbocharger wear, timing-component wear, and oil-consumption risk.

services.name: Engine Air Filter
materials: VIN-confirmed engine air filter; Mann C-series equivalent commonly available
steps: Locate the air box in the engine bay and remove the retaining Torx fasteners.
Lift the lid carefully without straining attached hoses or sensors.
Remove the old filter and clean loose debris from the lower housing.
Install the new element fully into the sealing channel.
Reinstall the lid evenly and tighten fasteners only until seated.
services.tips: Do not use an oiled aftermarket filter unless you have a specific reason and can avoid contaminating the mass-airflow sensing path.
services.whatItDoes: Filters inlet air before it reaches the turbocharger and engine.
services.whyItMatters: A clean, well-sealed filter protects the turbocharger compressor and engine from abrasive dust.
services.ifDelayed: Restriction can reduce airflow; a poor seal can allow dirt to damage turbocharger and engine components.

services.name: Cabin Air Filter
materials: Activated-charcoal cabin filter; confirm part number by VIN
steps: Empty the glove box and release its travel stops to lower it.
Locate the cabin-filter access cover behind the glove box.
Remove the cover and slide out the old filter.
Install the replacement with the airflow arrow oriented exactly as marked on the housing.
Reinstall the cover and glove box.
services.tips: Activated-charcoal filters cost slightly more but noticeably reduce outside odors and improve cabin comfort in traffic.
services.whatItDoes: Filters incoming HVAC air for dust, pollen, and odors.
services.whyItMatters: A clogged cabin filter reduces airflow and weakens A/C and windshield-defrost performance.
services.ifDelayed: Restricted HVAC airflow can impair visibility in humid weather and overwork the blower motor.

services.name: Aisin 8-Speed Automatic Fluid Service
tools: OBDeleven, VCDS, or scan tool capable of transmission-fluid temperature data
Fluid pump with correct transmission fill adapter
Level lift or four level jack stands
materials: New drain/fill sealing hardware as required
steps: Verify the exact transmission code and required fluid before purchase; do not assume an AWD transmission service kit applies to this FWD Tiguan.
Raise the vehicle level and remove the undertray for clear access.
Confirm the fill point can be opened before draining.
Drain the fluid using the factory overflow/standpipe procedure and measure the quantity removed.
Refill with the correct approved fluid, start the engine, cycle through gear positions, and monitor transmission-fluid temperature with a scan tool.
Set final level only within the factory temperature range, allowing excess fluid to drain to a controlled trickle.
Install plugs with required new seals, reinstall the undertray, road-test, and inspect for leaks.
services.tips: This is a temperature-controlled level-setting job. The scan tool is not optional if you want the correct final fluid level.
services.whatItDoes: Refreshes automatic-transmission fluid that lubricates internal gears and clutches and provides hydraulic control pressure.
services.whyItMatters: Correct fluid level and chemistry support shift quality, cooling, and transmission durability.
services.ifDelayed: Degraded or incorrectly leveled fluid can cause harsh shifts, delayed engagement, overheating, and internal clutch damage.

services.name: DOT 4 Brake Fluid Flush
tools: OBDeleven or VCDS recommended for ABS bleed functions
steps: Protect painted surfaces and fill the master-cylinder reservoir with fresh DOT 4 fluid.
Use a pressure bleeder and the factory wheel sequence.
If air entered the system or the ABS hydraulic unit was opened, use the scan-tool ABS bleed function before final manual bleeding.
Flush each corner until clean fluid appears, keeping the reservoir above the minimum mark.
Verify a firm pedal and correct fluid level before driving.
services.tips: Do not use DOT 3 as a substitute. A scan-tool ABS bleed is especially valuable after major brake-hydraulic repairs.
services.whatItDoes: Replaces moisture-contaminated brake fluid throughout the hydraulic system.
services.whyItMatters: DOT 4 fluid absorbs moisture with age, reducing boiling margin and increasing corrosion risk inside ABS components.
services.ifDelayed: High moisture content can cause soft-pedal feel, brake fade under repeated braking, and expensive ABS-system corrosion.

services.name: EA888 Spark Plug Inspection & Replacement
materials: Four VIN-confirmed OE-spec iridium spark plugs
steps: Remove the engine cover and ignition coils following the correct coil-connector release procedure.
Blow debris from each plug well before removing plugs.
Remove and inspect old plugs for oil fouling, abnormal deposits, or uneven wear.
Install VIN-confirmed plugs at the specified gap without applying anti-seize unless the plug manufacturer specifically directs it.
Torque plugs to the current factory specification, reinstall coils, and verify no misfire codes are present.
services.tips: Turbocharged direct-injection engines are less forgiving of worn plugs under boost. Use OE heat range and gap unless the vehicle is intentionally tuned for a different setup.
services.whatItDoes: Maintains reliable ignition of the pressurized air-fuel charge in the turbocharged engine.
services.whyItMatters: Correct plugs prevent misfires that can damage ignition coils, catalytic converters, and engine internals.
services.ifDelayed: Misfires under load can damage the catalytic converter and create coil failures or poor performance.

services.name: Intake-Valve Carbon Buildup Inspection
tools: Professional walnut-blasting equipment if cleaning is required
materials: Walnut-shell media and intake gaskets only if a cleaning service is warranted
steps: Scan for misfire, fuel-trim, and intake-related fault codes.
Evaluate symptoms such as rough cold starts, hesitation, recurring misfires, or reduced power.
If symptoms justify it, inspect intake valves with a borescope through an appropriate access point.
Use walnut-shell blasting or a qualified direct-injection intake-cleaning procedure only when actual buildup and symptoms warrant it.
services.tips: Do not add a generic fuel-tank additive expecting it to clean intake valves; direct injection does not wash fuel across the backs of the intake valves.
services.whatItDoes: Monitors a common direct-injection maintenance concern: deposits on intake valves.
services.whyItMatters: Heavy deposits can disrupt airflow and contribute to idle, performance, and misfire complaints.
services.estCost: $0-$50 inspect; $350-$700 if walnut blasting is needed
services.ifDelayed: If substantial deposits are ignored after symptoms begin, drivability problems and persistent misfires can worsen.

services.name: Cooling-System Inspection & Coolant Renewal
materials: VW-approved G12evo/G13-compatible coolant mixed only as specified
Replacement hose clamps or seals only if inspection finds leakage
steps: Inspect the expansion tank, water-pump area, thermostat housing, hoses, radiator, and underbody for dried coolant residue or active leaks.
Pressure-test the cooling system if coolant loss, odor, or visible residue is present.
If renewal is due, drain only when cold and refill with the exact Volkswagen-approved coolant chemistry.
Bleed air using the factory procedure, bring the engine to operating temperature, allow full cool-down, and set the final level.
services.tips: Do not mix coolants merely because their colors appear similar. Identify the existing coolant chemistry first.
services.whatItDoes: Maintains coolant freeze, boil, corrosion, and heat-transfer protection while checking common leak points.
services.whyItMatters: Cooling-system leaks or incorrect coolant can lead to overheating and damage to the water pump, thermostat housing, radiator, or engine.
services.ifDelayed: A slow leak can become an overheating event and cause severe engine damage.

services.name: FWD CV Axle, Wheel Bearing & Front Suspension Inspection
materials: None unless an axle, boot, wheel bearing, or suspension component is due
steps: Inspect both front CV boots for cracks, splits, and grease sling.
Check for clicking during tight low-speed turns and vibration during acceleration.
Check wheel bearings for looseness or roughness with the wheel unloaded.
Inspect front control-arm bushings, ball joints, tie rods, and strut mounts.
There is no rear driveshaft, rear differential, Haldex coupling, bevel box, or AWD fluid service on this FWD Tiguan.
services.tips: A torn CV boot is a repair-now item. Replacing a boot early is much cheaper than replacing the complete axle after the joint runs dry.
services.whatItDoes: Checks the FWD-specific components that transmit power to the front wheels and guide the front suspension.
services.whyItMatters: CV-joint or wheel-bearing failure can create noise, vibration, loss of drive, and unsafe handling.
services.ifDelayed: A damaged CV joint can fail completely; worn steering or suspension joints can affect tire wear and control.

services.name: Tire Rotation, Alignment & Brake Inspection
materials: None unless tires, pads, rotors, or alignment service are required
steps: Rotate tires in the pattern appropriate to their tread design and whether they are directional.
Torque lug bolts to the factory specification; verify against the owner's manual before service.
Measure tread depth across all tire positions and record uneven wear.
Inspect pads, rotors, caliper boots, and brake hoses.
Schedule a four-wheel alignment if the vehicle pulls, steering wheel is off-center, or tread wear is uneven.
services.tips: FWD vehicles often show faster front-tire wear. Consistent rotation is particularly important on this Tiguan.
services.whatItDoes: Manages tire wear while identifying brake and alignment concerns.
services.whyItMatters: Tires, brakes, and steering geometry are core safety systems.
services.ifDelayed: Uneven wear can destroy a tire early and hidden brake or suspension wear can worsen handling and stopping performance.
